Sessions/2026-01-13---Session-11.title: 2026-01-13 - Session 11
Sessions/2026-01-13---Session-11.content:
From the mouth of the basilisk’s cave, Wayne calls back that he can see a pink-and-white unicorn sitting beside a pond among the trees beyond.  As Arlo and Molly scramble up the steep climb into the basilisk cave, Numyr feels an odd tingle, like a cool breeze blowing through the cave.
The party steps out of the cave into the golden twilight of the marshy landscape beyond.
The fat little unicorn hauls itself to its feet and steps a few feet in their direction. Arlo opens the conversation with a polite introduction in the language of animals.  The unicorn shakes its head and replies in Common: “Are you speaking horse to me right now?”  It glances past them at the cave mouth and then at Numyr.  “And did you kill the guardian?”  It sighs and says, “I can see why she kills you.”
While apparently unimpressed with the party, the unicorn introduces himself as Pinky.  He is here on behalf of his lady, Maid Mareian.  She is a member of The Forking Path, a group of fey who observe the slowly unfolding paths of the future and nudges things, where they can, toward a more positive outcome.  Pinky identifies the party as “the Court of Jacks”.  He proceeds to name a courtly card for each member of the party: the Page of Cups to Numyr, the Knight of Wands to Arlo, the Queen of Pentacles to Molly, and the King of Swords to Wayne.  Pinky comments that they form a powerful court if they work together.
The party asks about the Troll Witch, but Pinky explains that she dwells primarily in the Mortal Realm. She is responsible for protecting some of the portals that lead from there to the Feywild, including the one behind them.  Some portals to the Feywild are hidden or hard to find; some are locked or barred; and some are guarded. This is done to keep mortals and their “mortality” in the Mortal Realm.  The Troll Witch set Old Krorg to guard this one.  Pinky’s expression softens a bit when he admits that the owlbear was fated to die soon by some means, even if the party hadn’t been the ones to do it.
Pinky also reveals that someone recently set the basilisk to guard this side of the portal, which is odd. It’s possible that she is responsible.  She has a name that Pinky refuses to speak aloud here “even if it’s not her real one”.  The basilisk is something Pinky is keeping an eye on, figuratively speaking.
Pinky reveals that the reason he was waiting for the party here was to pass along a prophesy. He recites:
When the king to gravity falls
While eight dark flames light gremkin halls,
The nightmares shall be held at bay
Until a child dies to those dark fey.
And so shall wait the queen of night
In her starlit lands, far from sight.
But if the flames to the queen doth come,
And draw their dark to aid her some,
Then with new strength the queen will lie,
drawing breath, while others die.
Gather your forces in days now near,
At the cost of those you will hold dear.
Pinky declines to offer any interpretation of these vague words. Since their path to the Troll Witch likely lies behind them, Pinky escorts them back to the cave entrance “before anyone sees”.  Then he sighs and say, “Well, before anyone else sees.”  Pixies are apparently hard to spot.
After bidding them farewell, Pinky passes behind a tree and does not emerge from the other side.
As the party passes back through the basilisk cave, they discuss the prophesy.  It sounds like there are two paths, but both are unclear and neither seems to be free of death.  But “gravity” and “the eight dark flames in gremkin halls” certainly seems to imply their current situation being some sort of turning point.  But a turning point to what exactly?  Who is this “queen”?
Emerging from Old Krorg’s cave, the party realizes a couple hours have suddenly passed and the sun is setting here.  They spot Gravity hopping down from the tree and packing away their book.  They approach and Molly takes lead. She and Gravity swap some gambling metaphors and some innuendo about the note Molly grabbed.  Gravity reiterates her offer: She’ll take them to the Grand Gremkin, protect them them from the gremkin horde, and lay no claim on any sol-fires found there; in exchange, the party will protect them from the Grand Gremkin.  After a quick huddle, the party shrugs and decides to go for it.  They accept Gravity’s offer.
Gravity leads them back into Old Krorg’s cave and reveals that the end of one of the tunnels can be walked through in total darkness.  Gravity says that there are many such portals to the Shadowfell.
On the other side, after a few twists and turns, the group comes upon a torch-lined stretch of tunnel that ends in an iron grate.  To the right is a short wooden door.  Gravity knocks, speaks the name of the little gremkin that opens it, and beckons the party into the gremkin warren.  The little gremkin is sure to close and bar the door after Wayne grabs one of the torches.  “Spiders”, the gremkin says.
In the main hall of the gremkin warren, surrounded by a hundred or more toothy goblins, the party meets the Grand Gremkin.  He is an obese bugbear lounging on a wooden throne, serviced in many different ways by the little gremkin around him.  Gravity approaches and explains their lineage, which makes them the bugbear’s grandchild, and thus a “Grand Grand Gremkin”.  While this wordplay seems to impress the gremkin crowd, it does not impress their king. When Gravity calls for the bugbear to relinquish his title or be slain by their companions, the Grand Gremkin struggles to his feet in outrage, calling for the death of these intruders.
Numyr dashes forward, holding the Grand Gremkin at bay with his shield.  In return, the goblin king summons a magical shield to his own arm and brings a great rusty morning star to bear.  While Gravity’s influence holds most of the gremkin at bay, the Grand Gremkin’s bellowed orders bring some of them to his aid.  They lift his great bulk over their heads, helping him to move quickly, but Wayne’s bag of ball bearings slows them significantly. They also seem to support the bugbear’s defense, fortifying his shield, which is able to deflect the energy of some blows into his minions instead.  Several gremkin rush forward, biting and gnawing at the party’s knees.
Although Arlo’s machetes clatter against the bugbear’s back and a thrust of Molly’s sword finds its way through his armor into his prodigious belly, a great blow from the Grand Gremkin’s morning star leaves Numyr’s ears ringing. The party is breathing hard in the thick humid dark of the warren as their torch flickers weakly on the floor.  Wayne draws his crossbow as a fresh swarm of gremkin rush out of the crowd to their king’s aid.

Sessions/2026-01-19---Session-12.title: 2026-01-19 - Session 12
Sessions/2026-01-19---Session-12.content:
Deep in the gremkin warren, the party is locked in combat with the Grand Gremkin.  Little biting gremkin loyalists swarm about their knees. At a word from their king, they toss his wooden throne at Numyr.  Arlo pushes it off of him, and he then helps Numyr up yet again when the Grand Gremkin fells him with a blow of his morning star.  The king’s shield turns Molly’s blade, but Wayne’s lasso and the surging crowd of spectator gremkin under Gravity’s sway keep the goblin king from retreating.
Finally, with a quick prayer to his god, Numyr brings a great killing blow down on the Grand Gremkin.  The obese bugbear falls to the cave floor, and Gravity lays claim to his title through the deeds of their knight champions.  The gremkin waver undecided at this shift in power, but Gravity draws a magic bronze fork from their satchel.  Laying this on a small rickety wooden table causes the table to magically expand into a great banquet spread.  The gremkin cheer and scramble, pushing and shoving, to their celebratory feast.
As the horde of little goblins is distracted, Gravity calls a few of them to help gather the eight sol fires for the party.  Gravity also introduces them to the former Grand Gremkin’s aged advisor, Pogus.  Pogus explains that the former Grand Gremkin collected the sol fires for the ruler of Evernight, whose name they do not speak aloud here.  When he says Evernight, he gestures towards the front door of the great hall.  The fires were to be collected by the Nightmare Brigade. He is nervous about what will happen when they don’t have the sol fires to turn over, but Gravity says they will all depart for the feywild in the morning.
With Gravity’s leave, the party loots the body of the former Grand Gremkin. Numyr takes his shield, which has the straps oddly attached to the face of the shield so that the bugbear could wear the shield backwards.  Arlo pockets a pouchful of fey coins.
As the party starts to ferry the sol fires out of the warren, Gravity gestures Molly over. Mollly admits to her name being on the slip of paper that Gravity had. Gravity asks Molly’s permission to test something.  Gravity pierces the tip of their own finger and then Molly’s with a slender silver dagger.  When the blood drops touch, blue and gold sparks fly.  Gravity exclaims that they are half-siblings and hugs Molly.  They each bear the elfin blood of the Seelie Court through their shared father.  Especially after exposure to the Feywild, Molly should be able to tap into the latent fey magic within her.
The party briefly considers both the door to Evernight and the iron grate to darkness at the end of the torch-lined hall, but they instead bid farewell to Gravity and carry the sol fires back the way they came.  They pass through the dark passage and pile the sol fires out front of Old Krorg’s cave. Wayne and Arlo make the journey to town across the dark prairie, collect a couple horses and some study pack harnesses, and return near midnight.  Together, the party transports the stolen sol fires back to Deadbridge.  They return the horses and sneak the sol fires up to their rooms.
To avoid any fey meddling, they set a watch through the night.  Those who watch during the darkest hours feel uneasy, like there might be a presence in the room. In the morning, there are no bedsheet knots or other physical signs of the killmoulis curse in their shared room, but none of the party have slept well.  It looks like a few nibbles were snuck from the food left on the wash basin, but no new message was left on the mirror or the paper note.
During the night, Numyr dreams of a fairy woman he recognizes.  She is gathering herbs in a wild field.  She congratulates Numyr on finding his way to her world, and welcomes him as her sweet page, come to be her knight.  She whispers her name to him, but then she saddens to see that he carries the mark of another.  She sings a song of seemingly impossible tasks that he might complete to prove he is a true knight of hers.  Before he can learn more, Molly wakes him for his turn at watch.
Similarly, Arlo dreams of being back in the Feywild, only this time he has Old Krorg’s blood on his hands.  When he shoves his hands into the soil to clean them, he feels a profound connection to the weave of life all around him. He then finds himself in a dream version of the prairie.  Even there, he feels a magical awareness of the land around him and the ability to call one of its members to him as a companion protector and guardian.  When Numyr wakes him for his turn at watch, Arlo is tempted to run into the pre-dawn light to reform the connection promised by the dream, but instead he takes his watch, hoping that he can reproduce that connection again another morning.
After sleeping in to nearly the end of breakfast, the party still feels tired and sore from their recent adventure.  As the men wait outside Molly’s door for her, they plan to stick close to home today and hopefully learn more from the killmoulis tonight if they don’t keep a watch. However, first they should return the sol fires.

Sessions/2026-01-27---Session-13.title: 2026-01-27 - Session 13
Sessions/2026-01-27---Session-13.content:
In the morning, when the party reunites outside of Molly’s room, they find that she has been visited by the killmoulis.  Along with fairy-locks in her hair and knots in her bedsheets, it left a message on Molly’s mirror:

shee iz in (th)e mowntins wi(th) trols and big horns
yu kilt (th)e beyr wi(th)owt asking
and yu ar not dooing yur job
sory butt iy am dooing my job
which yu mayk harder when yu stay awayk

While the rest of party guards the sol fires in their room, Wayne heads out to talk to the sheriff.  The sheriff appears skeptical of Wayne’s ramblings about a unicorn, a prophesy, and a Grand Gremkin, but she’s also just seen a gremkin horde attack her town. She directs Wayne to Wallop at the Luskan Mining Company.
Wayne walks down to the mining company warehouse and introduces himself to Wallop.  The young gobber is pleased to hear that the sol-fires were recovered, and he agrees to Wayne’s suggestion that they should chain the fires in place.  Wallop says he can bring a wagon down to the hotel to pick them up.
Wayne also meets Wallop’s kid sister, Smyrna, who is lurking behind her “Apothecandy” stand.  Wayne buys a chocolate hopper sampler.  A gleeful Smyrna leads him outside to try it, and  Wayne leaps 30 feet into the air.
Meanwhile, back at the hotel, Numyr is playing around with the grand gremkin’s shield.  He invites Arlo, who is fletching some fresh crossbow bolts, to try shooting him.  Things escalate quickly, and a moment later there is a shattered window.  Then Numyr feels dizzy, like he has a couple times before.  He has a faint sense of being back in Old Krorg’s cave.   Then he feels over two hundred individual stabs of pain into his head over the next several minutes.  He cries out and collapses to the ground.  Mary Wilcox knocks on their door to find out what is happening, and Molly keeps her at bay.
Wayne returns just as Numyr’s fit of agony is tapering off. The party loads the sol fires into Wallop’s wagon out front, and then Wayne talks Mary into giving them a late breakfast. Numyr is pale but mostly recovered.  They slip out to the side of the hotel to collect what window shards they can so they can mend it later, and they all pay for another night.
Wayne then leads them back down the street to see Smyrna’s apothecandy stand.  Wallop warns them that Smyrna is only six, but he also quietly admits that she is brilliant and that her candy is probably generally safe now that is has gone through “faze 1” (do no harm) and “faze 2” (it works) testing.  After signing an informed consent form in crayon, the party samples the peanut ears, the loquacious licorice, and the bubble rocks. Molly finds their first “oopsie” when her mouth turns inky black from the licorice.  They each get two samples of each of the test candies to take with them.
As they leave the workshop, they notice four rough-looking men talking to the sheriff.  Given their cocky stances, the party ambles in their direction to overhear more.  Wayne catches a snippet of conversation in a familiar voice and recognizes one of the men from behind.  Wayne quickly cuts behind the carpenter’s shed and gestures for the party to carry on without him.
The men work for Frank Lawrence, some person of influence in Laramie, to the southwest.  He is pushing to form a Lawrence Territory, including Laramie, Caspar, and Deadbridge.  His men, who serve as deputies in Laramie, were riding through town and stopped to chat with the sheriff, reveling in the fact that they’ll all be working together in law enforcement once the territory is established.
Sheriff Catherine relaxes slightly when Molly, Arlo, and Numyr step over in a quiet gesture of support. The jocular leader introduces himself as Leroy, and Molly says he can call her Al. He invites her for a drink, but her refusal (and a glimpse of her ink-stained mouth) largely kills the mood.  Frank Lawrence’s men take their horses across the street and head into the Velvet Palace.
The sheriff gives them a thankful nod.  They reunite with Wayne, who explains that he recognized one of the men as a member of a gang he once knew.  Since he killed their leader, he wanted to avoid any subsequent violence if he were recognized in return.
Back in their room, Numyr continues to practice with the shield, revealing some of its magical properties.  Arlo slips out in the late afternoon and doesn’t return until well after dark, smelling of earth, grass, and something else that’s not quite horse.
As the party turns in for the night, Numyr leaves a fresh note for the killmoulis:

Tel her we sory, want owt, com tawk to her riyt away
how do we fiynd her?

Rewards

Two each of the three testing apothecandies: bubble rocks, loquacious licorice, and peanut ears.

XP









































AmountToForTotal+40ALL+20 for returning the sol-fires; +10 for joining “faze 3 apothecandy trials”; +10 for helping the Sheriff face down Frank Lawrence’s men (and avoiding greater complications for Wayne)590Wayne620Numyr620Molly620Arlo620

Sessions/2026-02-17---Session-14.title: 2026-02-17 - Session 14
Sessions/2026-02-17---Session-14.content:
Before turning in for the night, Numyr eats a very meager dinner. Wayne steps out to talk to Sancho, hiring him to come along and mind their horses for the upcoming journey.
In the morning, there is a fresh message scratched on the wash basin mirror:

go to the big horn mowntins
(th)ree days to (th)e nor(th)west
she lives by (th)e grayt medisin wheel

The party purchases some extra rations, meets up with Sancho, and heads out of town. Arlo lugs his saddle with him for the first hour or so.  Then, from over a rise, appears a powerful buffalo, which trots down to Arlo and nuzzles its great nose into Arlo’s palm with a snort.  Arlo introduces Tatanka to the party and saddles up.
Through the first day, Wayne’s left boot feels tight and uncomfortable.  But when he checks it and his feet, everything looks normal.
Just after dusk on the first night, Arlo’s watch is interrupted by a shuffling creature approaching in the dark.  After shouting a warning, Arlo dismembers the skeletal undead gnoll. It was a little confused but still aggressive. The dismembered corpse is dirty, smells like death, and its bare skull had previously been smashed in.  Arlo and Numyr backtrack it a little ways in the dark; it seems to have come alone from the east.   Everyone’s watch is tense through the rest of the night.
On the second day, Molly’s right boot now feels tight and off-balance.  Numyr is still not hungry, but he forces himself to eat a little.  As they cross the wide prairie, they pass a number of depopulated prairie dog towns. When they stop for lunch, Arlo approaches one of the few remaining prairie dogs and communes with it.  From the images he magically receives from it, Arlo learns that the prairie dog tunnels have gradually filled with a cold implacable darkness.  While sometimes a prairie dog could push on through the darkness to emerge in the far end of a tunnel, more and more prairie dogs disappeared into that dark.  The description reminded Arlo of pushing through the darkness to go to the gremkin warren.  The fur of the prairie dogs that remain has became streaked with black, and their sleep is haunted by a dread of spider-like predators in the dark.
On the third day of the journey, the contours of the Bighorns Mountains sharpen into detail ahead.  As the party nears the foothills, they spy eight other riders approaching from the north.  The riders turn to intercept the party.  As they approach, the party sees that they are buffalo riders, like Arlo.  They begin to whoop and charge, but Wayne recognizes their Orcish cries and calls out to them.  He is able to calm them enough that they approach.  They want the party gone from their lands because humans bring darkness and death with them. Wayne assures them that his group will pass through quickly to the mountains and then return from whence they came. He is able to get rough directions to the Medicine Wheel—whatever that is—near the white peak ahead.  When asked whether the party has seen any gnolls, which the orcs are hunting, Wayne points them to the skeletal one they killed two nights ago. The orcs nod grimly to each other when they learn that the gnoll was undead. They remind the party not to linger, look Arlo and Tatanka up and down with something like approval, whoop and cry, and ride off to the south.
Noon is approaching and it looks like the foothills of the Bighorn Mountains are now only a couple hours away.
Rewards
XP









































AmountToForTotal+20ALLLearning that gnolls are also facing an undead problem (+5), that the prairie dogs tunnels are filling with shadow and loss (+5), and that orcs think that humans bring death with them to these lands (+10)610Wayne640Numyr640Molly640Arlo640

Sessions/2026-02-24---Session-15.title: 2026-02-24 - Session 15
Sessions/2026-02-24---Session-15.content:
The party’s three-day journey across the prairie brings them to the base of the Bighorn Mountains. As they make their way into the foothills along an animal trail, they spot three trolls watching them.
When the party approaches, the trolls spread out and block the way forward.  Two of the trolls are larger, with tusks protruding from their lower jaws.  The middle troll is smaller. He wears a black vest and squashed hat, both a little too tight for him.  This smaller troll speaks some Common. When Numyr steps forward, the troll bars the way, claiming that the Troll Witch doesn’t need to be disturbed for “conversation” with such a puny creature. When Numyr suggests a little duel, the troll grins like that is exactly what he was hoping for.
Numyr steps back to confer with the party, then he opens with a witchbolt on the the troll. The troll rushes Numyr and rips into him.  Meanwhile, the troll’s companions languidly stroll up to watch the fight and to keep an eye on the rest of the party.
Numyr lands a few more blows on the troll, but the slashes heal closed nearly instantly.  Numyr fends the troll off with his shield, eyeing for an opening where he might lop off an arm.  However, before he finds one, the troll throws both of his long arms around Numyr and tries to bite his face.  With a magical whisper, accompanied by the scent of mint and patchouli, Numyr charms the troll for a moment, getting him to admit that Numyr has achieved the upper hand.
When the troll then shakes his head clear of the enchantment a moment later, he reconsiders. However, he is now on the back foot after learning that Numyr can do magic.  The troll sulks, claiming that he, Billy the Kid, would win any fair fight.  Nevertheless, the three trolls still lead the party deeper into the hills toward the white peak.
At the base of a narrow stony climb, Wayne stations a trembling Sancho to guard Betty and Tatanka.  At the top of the climb, beside a great stone circle, the trolls point the party to a small hut nestled in a shady grove of trees.
Numyr’s knock at the door is answered by a grumbling voice from within.  The Troll Witch opens the door. Once Numyr explains why he is there, she eventually invites everyone in.  Although she has no seats for them, she makes them all mudroot tea in clay mugs.
Regarding Numyr’s guardianship, she first suggests that he just let her grandson Billy tear his arms off and thus replace Numyr as the new guardian.  However, when that plan does not sound agreeable, she offers up a rhyming recipe that would break her enchantment.

Find me an acre of land between the sea and the wet beach sand
Plow it with a ram’s white horn and sow it all with a lone peppercorn
Harvest it with a sickle of leather, and wrap the bounty in a peacock feather
Bring it to me, damp with sea spray, and you’ll be free with no price to pay

Further discussion reveals that the killmoulis has followed them from Deadbridge, hidden in their boots.  The Troll Witch bangs the killmoulis out of Molly’s boot and calls him Sphinctus. Sphinctus is rather upset to be both revealed and named before the party, but he contributes the fact that there is now a basilisk guarding the other side of the portal.  Numyr relates that there is also a second portal through the dark of the cave to Evernight.
Recognizing that there is much more going on with this particular portal than she realized, the Troll Witch ponders where she could get a more trustworthy and capable guardian.  Calling Numyr over to her cauldron, she scries on a canyon not far from Deadbridge.  Occupied by several ogres, the back cave holds an imprisoned ogress named Mora. Mora was a student or disciple of the Troll Witch, but she made a very bad relationship decision in choosing her mate, Blorugg.  The ogre brute has since gouged out her eyes and now keeps her prisoner.
The Troll Witch gives Numyr a small magical egg.  With a drop of his own blood applied to it, he can give it to Mora to consume and his guardianship will thus be passed to her.  The Troll Witch says not to mention her name to anyone else in relation to Mora’s rescue.
Numyr also asks about his starving curse.  The Troll Witch guesses that it is due to his magical shield, and that he should seek either wizardly or eladrin advice on such things.  She also spots that he has the blessing of another fey patron and comments that she hates getting pulled into Seelie fey politics.
With a path now clear to breaking at least Numyr’s guardian curse, the party bids the Troll Witch farewell.  With Sphinctus transferred to Molly’s pack instead of her boot, they pass by the trolls and return to Sancho at the base of the hill.
As they spend three nights traveling back to Deadbridge, they learn that Sphinctus the killmoulis would prefer to be called Tom.  Now that the Troll Witch has let them off the hook for killing the owlbear, he is happy to repay their many kindnesses with exceptionally restful sleep and a few minor cleaning tasks around the camp.
On a late Sunday afternoon, the party rides back into Deadbridge.
Rewards
XP









































AmountToForTotal+150ALLPassing the trolls (+30); negotiating a path forward with the Troll Witch (+50); ending the killmoulis curse (+50); befriending the killmoulis (+10); keeping Sancho alive on the journey (+10)760Wayne790Numyr790Molly790Arlo790

Sessions/2026-03-03---Interlude-1A.title: 2026-03-03 - Interlude 1A
Sessions/2026-03-03---Interlude-1A.content:
The Shaggy Man, played using Don’t Rest Your Head rules.
Benny, Debbie, Wendy, and Ailbhe arrive for school at the Deadbridge schoolhouse. The night before, the shadowy figure of the Shaggy Man threatened each of them in their beds. Although they pulled the covers up over their heads, the morning revealed a single blood spot on their upper blanket and the sky had changed.  Now, the starry night fills the heavens above, even at midday.
At school, they notice one of their classmates, Petey, is missing. They decide to go check on him to make sure the Shaggy Man didn’t get him.  While Benny and Wendy charge forward and Debbie waits for her, Aibhe asks Sister Charlotte for any homework to take to Petey.
When the two lagging girls catch up with Benny and Wendy, they are discussing the wagon on the other side of Main Street.  The wagon is small, brightly covered, and built to house a single traveler on a long road.  It is festooned with bunches of dried lavender, which an oddly-dressed peddler next to the wagon is selling to people walking past on the boardwalk. But the most noteworthy part of the wagon is the black mule hitched to the front of it.  Wisps of smoke rise from its hooves, and its eyes glow like red embers.  None of the passersby seem to notice.
After considering whether to throw a rock at it, the children settle a more cunning plan. Wendy marches up to the peddler and engages him in rambling conversation. The peddler smiles and offers her a sample of lavender. Ailbhe approaches the nightmare mule and discovers that it can talk in a deep voice, although it’s not very friendly. While the peddler is keeping an eye on both of the girls, Debbie keeps a lookout while Benny sneaks up to the side of the wagon opposite the peddler. He peeks in through its little window.  The wagon’s interior seems mundane, with no signs of Petey.  Benny gives the all-clear, and the children regroup further down the street.  The peddler watches them go, sniffing the sprig of lavender still in his hand and unconsciously fingering the burlap sack that hangs from his belt within the folds of his long robe-like poncho.
Hurrying on to Petey’s house, the children catch brief sight of a white face in the upstairs window.  Petey’s mother answers the door to their knock. Although she looks weary and is hesitant to welcome them in, Wendy pushes a little bit with her convincing chatter. Although Ma-Petey starts to cry a little, she lets them all in and points them upstairs to Petey’s bedroom.
They find Petey sitting in a ball on his bed. He has lost of a forefinger to the Shaggy Man. He poked it out from under his covers to get a little air and, with the glistening flash of a black knife, it was gone.  Petey can also see the daytime night sky, but he feels that the Shaggy Man now has a strong hold over him.  Petey can sense the Shaggy Man moving through shadows somewhere nearby.  He can hear rattling chains and the moans of lost souls.
The children put their heads together.  Obviously the Shaggy Man is a creature of darkness, so light should stop him.  They come up with the plan of “borrowing” a string of thermalights and a sol-fire to power them. They’ll set this up in Petey’s room and flick it on when the Shaggy Man comes.  They can all hide under the covers of Petey’s bed if it doesn’t work.
Bidding farewell to a cheered Petey, they step out onto the street.  Looking upward for strings of the artificial lights, they spot four laughing blue fairies whiz by overhead.  Debbie runs after them and sees them disappear under a woodshed.  She and Benny approach, and Benny dives under the woodshed to catch one in his baseball glove. One of the fairies bounces into the pocket of his glove, but it wriggles free. With a shriek, the fairies drop the stolen candy they were eating and bolt in all directions in a burst of blue fairy dust.  Benny and Debbie both sway from the slumbering effect, but one of the fairies lingers, hovering over their heads.
The fairy is surprised that they can see it.  When asked about the creature hunting them, the fairy asks whether it has a sack or a knife. Either way, the fairy doesn’t spend time with such beings.  When asked about what household items, like garlic or onions, might keep the the knife-wielding Shaggy Man away, the fairy says such household concerns belong to a killmoulis, not to a fairy. It comments that Nocturna will be unhappy to have human children wandering in the Evernight… or perhaps very happy. It recommends that the children “pick a side”.
Suddenly realizing that its companions have flown off, the fairy cuts the conversation short with these grabby human children. It zips away toward Main Street.
The children glance at each other and realize their plan hasn’t really changed. It’s time to steal a string of thermalights.

Sessions/2026-03-10---Interlude-1B.title: 2026-03-10 - Interlude 1B
Sessions/2026-03-10---Interlude-1B.content:
The Shaggy Man, played using Don’t Rest Your Head rules, continues.
Wendy, Benny, Debbie, and Ailbhe stroll from Petey’s house down to Main Street, looking at the thermalights and chained sol-fires. There are none that could be easily swiped, so they follow the string of lights across the bridge and head for the mine.
A miner there blocks them from going in. Aibhe says she’s there to see her father, “Jim Dawson”, and so the miner steps back into the mine to look for him.  Aibhe follows him, hoping to either duck into the shadows or casually stand aside if the miner should return.  However, the shadows start gathering in around her, and she chokes.  She runs back to the light and the rest of her friends waiting for her on the bridge.
The children cross back over the bridge to see if there might be any other sol-fires they’d overlooked.  Poking their head into the Mining Company workshop, Wendy starts a conversation with Wallop the gobber.  She learns that there aren’t any handy sol-fires around and that the sturdy stone storage shed in the center of the mining company courtyard holds excavated phi-stones awaiting shipment. When Wendy then fights back some tears over her fear of the coming dark, Wallop makes her a small nightlight.
Still hopeful to find keys to the sol-fire padlocks or more information about the mine, they head to the mining office.  Again Wendy takes lead, aiming to distract the mining director, Walter Movart.  Wendy exclaims that she just saw a little goblin messing with one of the sol-fires, so Movart hauls himself to his feet.  Escorting Wendy, he stops by the workshop to collect Wallop and then they head to the Sheriff’s office.
During the hub-bub, Debbie and Benny hang back.  Debbie talks Benny into breaking the office window, and then he stands guard while she slips in to look around.  She doesn’t find much regarding the sol-fires, but she does see a large map of the mine and the shift schedule, which shows that the mine will be clear this evening.  Benny has to run a distraction op with a “lost dog” story as a miner passes by so that Debbie can slip out again unseen.
Meanwhile, Wendy leads Ailbhe, Movart, Wallop, and the sheriff down to the water tank and the sol-fire that powers the pump there.  Wendy successfully plies her tale of the little gremkin.  Aibhe sadly interjects that gremkin killed her mother.  In the awkward silence following, the Sheriff says she will set up a patrol for the night to make sure there aren’t more in the area.
As the townsfolk return to their jobs, the party regroups behind the hotel and discusses their next move.  The mine seems like their best option, but it’s nearly dinner time.  They separate and head home for now to eat and check in with their parents.
A couple hours later, they meet back at the bridge.  They cross over to the mine, which is now locked shut.  However, the gap under the entrance grate is just wide enough for them to squeeze under.
Inside, they find that there is a small sol-fire in the first large room of the mine. It is chained to an iron spike driven into the stone. Using a crowbar and Debbie’s help, Benny is able to pry the spike loose.  Both are breathing hard from the effort, remembering how long it has been since they slept. They pull the sol-fire out of the niche it is in and disconnect its string of lights.  They start pulling the light string down from the line of nails it is sitting on.
As they do, the thermalights further into the mine begin to flicker and dim.  Glancing in that direction, they see an enormous spider—bigger than any of them—drifting down the tunnel toward them!
Wendy flips on her her nightlight and starts running back toward the entrance, leading the way.  Debbie runs behind her, pulling down the string of thermalights and gathering them in her arms.  Aibhe hauls the sol-fire, while Benny turns back to the face the spider.
After drawing a trading card from his pocket, Benny gives a dragon-like roar.  The shadow of a long neck and and a jet of flame seems to flicker around him as he lays into the spider with his baseball bat.
The spider stumbles to one side, but it also knocks past Benny to shoot webbing after Aibhe.  However, as the webbing floats to the ground, it reveals that Aibhe is no longer there.  Benny hauls himself back to his feet, swinging his bat again.  The spider checks itself and scurries backwards.  Aibhe steps out of a shadowy alcove.  As the spider retreats, she and Benny turn and run after the others.
Everyone scrambles under the mine gate. They are just barely able to shove the small sol-fire through with a shriek of metal.
Sunset is now at hand, and so nightfall is not far behind. Clutching their stolen sol-fire and bundle of thermalights, they head for Petey’s.

Sessions/2026-03-17---Interlude-1C.title: 2026-03-17 - Interlude 1C
Sessions/2026-03-17---Interlude-1C.content:
The Shaggy Man, played using Don’t Rest Your Head rules, concludes.
Wendy, Benny, Debbie, and Ailbhe stand just outside the Deadbridge mine as dusk falls. They hide the string of stolen thermalights in Debbie’s school bag and throw a sack that Debbie brought over the sol-fire.  They haul them to Petey’s house.
Once there, they hide in the bushes out front while Benny gives Ailbhe a boost up to the roof over the doorstep.  From there, Ailbhe sneaks into Petey’s room through the window he left ajar.  She can hear Petey and his mom cleaning up after dinner, so she hides in Petey’s closet.
Once his mom has tucked him in and left a nightlight candle burning, Ailbhe whispers to Petey from his closet.  Petey gives a yelp!  After his mother has checked on him again, Ailbhe sneaks downstairs to the front door to let the others in with the heavy gear.  She has to slip through the shadows as Petey’s mom tidies up for the night.  Ailbha begins to panic as the shadows start to close on in her again, but this time she pushes her way through the fear, actually daring the shadows to come at her. When they do, she pulls them in to disguise and muffle her friends as the group climbs the stairs to Petey’s room.
There, they make some plans and move some furniture, such as Petey’s trunk and hat rack. They then settle down to wait, with Benny under a blanket at the base of the bed and the rest of them under the covers by Petey’s knees.  Petey blows out the candle just in time so that his mother does not get a good view of the several lumps under his covers when she checks on him before turning in.  Soon the house grows quiet.
Suddenly everyone feels it: There is a presence in the room.  Benny hears the creak of the floorboards and a rustle as Petey pulls the blankets up over his head.
Then Benny launches their plan into action. He leaps up from under his blanket and throws the lid open on Petey’s trunk.  The glowing thermalights fill the room with light.  The Shaggy Man, standing next to the bed, raises one hand to shield his eyes while he brings the other down at Benny.  That hand bears a curved black blade.  However, Benny is already onto phase two of the plan.  The blade thunks into the wooden floor behind him as he sprints forward, the end of the thermalight string in one hand, rounding the assorted bases of laundry they’d laid out on the bedroom floor.
As the string of lights closes around the Shaggy Man, shadows stream from his form.  He stumbles back, abandoning his blade, and leaps for the closet door.  He passed through the door with a burst of shadow.
Benny, still mad from his sprint, chases after him, but wrenching open the closet door reveals only hanging clothes.
The children regroup.  Petey can still feel the Shaggy Man, not far off.  The Shaggy Man is a little shaken from their ambush but very angry too. They’ve rattled him, but he’s still a threat.  They have his knife now, but the handle is made of insubstantial shadow while the blade is like sharp obsidian.
Not sure what to do, they stow the lights back into the trunk and lie in the dark, whispering possible plans to each other.  Eventually they fall silent for a while.
In that silence, they hear light scurrying footfalls and a quiet voice muttering to itself.  Benny throws open the trunk again, revealing a foot-high figure with an enormous nose and a small rag, which it is using to polish at the knife gouge in the floor.
Once it realizes the children can see him, the old killmoulis introduces himself as Nedward.  Puzzling over how they can see him, he conjectures it is because they are caught between the worlds.  As he put is, if the Shadow realm is one slice of bread and the Mortal realm another, then the children are now caught in Evernight, the peanut butter that forms the sandwich between the two.  The bogeyman they call the Shaggy Man likely did this to them.  However, because he didn’t pay the cost of pulling them into the Evernight peanut butter, they gained the energy of that transfer as special powers.  The Shaggy Man will likely continue to haunt the children, bleeding that energy off them of them in some way to his own ends, such as using it to form his own portal so that he can fully enter the Evernight or even haunt the Mortal realm.
The children try to buy old Nedward’s help with promises of treats like porridge, butter, milk, or raisins, but the killmoulis adamantly refuses to get involved in such fey politics.  Although he doesn’t have anyone else to recommend, he points out that the children could follow the Shaggy Man through the closet themselves.
They get out of bed, pull Petey after them, disconnect the thermalights, and push the trunk into the closet.  When they close the door and feel around the back wall in the pitch black, they find that they can shove their way through the clothes into another closet.  When they open the door of that closet, they find themselves in a ruined shadowy version of Petey’s parents’ room.  Through gaping holes in the wall, they see a featureless black sky.  The air is filled with a faint blue light with no apparent source.
Through the holes in the wall, Wendy hears moaning and a rattle of chains drift up from the ground floor. She slips over and peeks down through the broken window.  A grey-skinned white-haired child looks up at her with huge haunted eyes.  The child holds up her hands in pleading supplication, revealing that she is missing a forefinger.  A chain dangles from a manacle around her wrist.  When Wendy steps back from the window in alarm, the child panics and begins to mournfully moan.
At that, the group hears something large get to its feet around the corner, in what would be Petey’s room.  They quickly spring into action.  Benny starts to hammer the shadow blade into his bat, but, on this side of the closet, it is the blade that is insubstantial instead of the handle.  He starts strapping it on to the bat instead.
Wendy runs to the bedroom door and closes it.  The door has been slashed open at some point, leaving wide jagged cracks running down its face.  Through the cracks, she can see the Shaggy Man step from Petey’s room.  Wendy begins to quail, playing that she is alone and lost and defenseless.  The Shaggy Man grins and strides forward.
As he does, Debbie and Ailbhe shove the trunk against the closed bedroom door.  The Shaggy Man peers in at them through the cracks, growling and befuddled to learn that there are more of them.  With a roar, he smashes open the top half of the door and starts to climb over the bottom half.
Debbie sends her hair forward in a burst.  It wraps around his neck, pins his arms, grips the door frame, and pushes him back.  As the Shaggy Man gags in the doorway, Ailbhe darts forward, throws back the lid of the trunk, and screws in the thermalights.  The Shaggy Man reaches a clawed hand out for her, but Ailbhe fades into the shadows.
And then Benny rounds the corner, hefting his blade-strapped baseball bat to his shoulder.  With a flick of a trading card from his fingers,  the spectral wings of a dragon appears in the shadows on the ceiling over his head.  He steps up onto a corner of the trunk; the thermalights stream their light up into the face of the entangled Shaggy Man; and Benny swings as hard as he can for home.
His bat gives a resounding crack as it smashes through the Shaggy’s Man face. The bogeyman’s body drops to the floor.
Quickly, not knowing if this might shift the balance of energies at play or draw other players to this part of the stage, the party disconnects the lights and shuts the trunk.  As they slide it back to the closet, Wendy pulls a necklace of fingers from the Shaggy Man’s chest.  One of the fingers looks fresh, so she gives it to Petey, who helplessly holds it against his severed knuckle.  Wendy tosses the rest of string to the pale child out the window.  The child seems too far gone for speech, but her face grows tranquil as she looks down at the fingers she holds in her hands.
The party pulls the closet door closed behind them, and they shove their way back through the hanging clothes.  The clothes seem denser this time, but they push harder and soon open Petey’s closet door.
They are home again.  Petey lets go of the finger and holds up his hand.  It is whole, his finger restored.
After catching their breaths, the children realize that they lost the blade somewhere on the other side of the closet. They bid farewell to Petey and slip out of the house.  They leave the sol-fire and the thermalights by the mining company warehouse, where they will be found by Wallop in the morning.  Then they hurry home and fall into their own beds, exhausted.  Blessedly, sleep comes close behind.
In the morning, the sky has returned to normal.  At school, they share broad smiles. Wendy passes Petey a jar filled with porridge, liberally sprinkled with raisins.

Sessions/2026-03-31---Session-16.title: 2026-03-31 - Session 16
Sessions/2026-03-31---Session-16.content:
The party returns to Deadbridge in the late afternoon.
Numyr hurries over to Old Max’s shop and pays the sage to inspect his Shield of the Grand Gremkin.  The wizard explains that the original magic has been “bent” when its original effect was inverted, but he should be able to “straighten it out” into one configuration or the other and thus remove the shield’s hunger-related side-effects.  Numyr negotiates with Old Max that he will bring him news and samples of the fey and fairytale creatures and objects that he finds in exchange for cheaper services from Max.  Old Max agrees to the arrangement and will charge Numyr near cost for his services (about half his normal price). At the new rate, he removes the curse from Numyr’s shield.
The party eats dinner at the hotel and cleans the dust of prairie travel from themselves, then each falls into bed with a sigh.  They leave out some snacks for Tom the killmoulis and gain a restful night’s sleep.
In the morning, after a hotel breakfast, they head west toward the canyon that the Troll Witch scried for Numyr.  As they pass the Sheriff’s Office on the way out of town, a happy but hungover Sancho waves them down to share some interesting new gossip:

A couple days after they left town, a child spotted a gremkin trying to steal one of chained sol fires in broad daylight.
That night, one of the sol-fires and its attached lights were moved from the mine to the mining company yard.  That was probably just a miner, but it was noted given the earlier gremkin sighting.
A different child’s mother said in church that her son lost his finger but it was miraculously healed.

After a morning’s walk (or ride for Wayne and Arlo), the party reaches the canyon.  There is a crude totem of skulls at the narrow entrance, piles of thorny brush, and a dusty midden pile of skins, bones, and torn clothing.
The party keeps their distance while Wayne and Arlo climb up the nearby rock face and work their way over the scrub-covered ridge line to peer into the canyon.  Arlo nearly knocks a rock tumbling down the slope ahead of him, but he catches it and freezes in place.  Wayne creeps down over the crest until he can hear two ogres talking in a thick guttural language just below him.  He can see piles of brush, scattered rocks, and broken bones in the canyon.  In the back wall, a large cave mouth leads into darkness.
Rather than risking approaching closer, Wayne and Arlo fall back to discuss the plan with the rest of the party.  A frontal assault on two ogres, with more unaccounted for in the nearby cave, seems risky. Setting a fire or waiting for darkness doesn’t seem to offer much in their favor. The best idea they have at the moment is to hide near the canyon entrance while Arlo’s Tatanka limps into the canyon.  If luck is with them, that might lure the two ogres out of the canyon and into an ambush.
Rewards
XP









































AmountToForTotal+10ALLReturning Deadbridge and learning of the gremkin spotting; (Numyr breaks his shield curse)770Wayne800Numyr800Molly800Arlo800

Sessions/2026-04-07---Session-17.title: 2026-04-07 - Session 17
Sessions/2026-04-07---Session-17.content:
As the party hides themselves in an alcove in the cliff face just outside Bad Canyon, Tantaka limps into the canyon as a distraction. After a couple taking a hits from hurled rocks, Tatanka rounds the corner again at a run with two large ogres in close pursuit.
Numyr steps out and holds the line in front of the larger ogre. Molly snipes him over Numyr’s head while Arlo charges him on Tatanka.  Wayne scrambles up the cliff face behind them to snipe from above.
The younger ogre brother moves in to hit Molly, and Wayne shoves a boulder down the cliff onto him.  Then, with a groan and meaty thumb, the older ogre collapses to a quick pommel blow from Numyr.  Numyr pops one of Smyrna’s loquacious licorices. His breath suddenly smells like hot tar and creosote, but he is able to communicate with the younger ogre.  He tell him to haul his brother away if he doesn’t want to see him killed.
The younger ogre, already surprised to see his mighty brother felled, drags his unconscious brother back by the wrist. With the way forward now clear, the party advances into the canyon.
As they approach the cave on the back wall, the ogre behind them hollers a warning.  Molly eats a peanut ear.  She suddenly goes deaf, but she finds she can hear people’s thoughts if she focuses intently on them.
When they reach the cave, Numyr peers into the dark and sees a large ogre slipping into cover behind a wall of thorny brush.  Numyr advances, shield at the ready, and the ogre charges him, swinging a small tree trunk driven through with old swords and bits of metal.
The hulking ogre then retreats back into the darkness of the cave, but Numyr pursues.  When he casts an arcing witchbolt, he draws the ogre out once more, but the ogre lays into three of the party with a great swing of his tree-trunk club.  Wayne lassos him. Arlo charges him on Tatanka and peppers him with arrows.  Molly shoots him, and then rushes forward to stab him, but she is knocked down by his club.  Numyr calls a healing word to her and she crawls back to her feet.
In the distraction of battle, Wayne lights the wall of brush aflame and dashes past the surrounded ogre into the darkness of the cave.  He finds a young female ogre chained near a large pile of bedding behind the brush.  Wayne tosses her a crowbar to free herself, and then he shoots back at the hulking ogre, who is now surrounded by the rest of the party and silhouetted against the entrance of the cave.  Blorugg, cruel Pappy of the ogre clan, falls dead.
As smoke from the burning brush begins to fill the cave, the young ogress pulls her chain spike free of the stone.  Wayne and Numyr hurry past a second wall of brush to find Mora—blinded, disfigured, and imprisoned—in a back room of the cave.
Numyr quickly explains why they have come, and he hands her the bloodied egg. Although she pauses for a moment, considering his words, she eats the egg.  Her eyes and fingers heal, and her tongue reforms.  When she reaches out to lay a hand on Numyr, he feels painfully dizzy for a moment as something passes between them.
Once Mora has freed herself from her own chain and has grabbed a few essential supplies from a stash in the back of the cave, she and her daughter flee with party back into the open air.
At the cave mouth, Mora spits on Blorugg’s corpse and scans the empty canyon. She is relieved to learn that the party has not slain any of her children.  (Molly catches her daughter, Sheeka, explaining that the youngest, Grunt, was killed by unknown humans a few days previously.)
Over a shared snack of rations and water, Numyr describes where Old Krorg’s portal cave lies. Mora says she will contact the Troll Witch for more instructions.  Mora’s conscious son, Little Grod, hollers again from the prairie beyond the canyon, and she yells for him to come in.
With the remains of the ogre family reunited, the party takes their leave.  On the way out, Wayne spots a couple drifting slips of paper.  They prove to be 10 notes. Quickly slipping over to the shallow cave where the ogre brothers slept, the party finds a torn leather wallet with several more.  They pocket 240 in total.  From the torn and bloody scraps of clothing scattered in the ogre’s cave, they can tell that several humans have died badly here.
They hurry out of Bad Canyon, past the snoring body of Big Tod.  Once they are some distance from the canyon, they pause to tend to the worst of their wounds. Then they turn once more for Deadbridge.
Rewards

240 in 24 x 10 notes

XP









































AmountToForTotal+250ALLRescuing Mora and passing on Numyr’s guardianship (+200), without killing any (more) of her children (+50)1020Wayne1050Numyr1050Molly1050Arlo1050

Wanted-Board/index.title: index
Wanted-Board/index.content:
The Wanted Board hangs just outside the Sheriff building in Deadbridge.
Originally hung to hold official Wanted posters, most of the board has been taken over by unofficial postings from other townsfolk.
Other Leads
Aside from the Wanted Board, the party has learned some other potential avenues of exploration.  The threads that are still open include:

The mine (lifeblood of the town) is facing several issues - 2025-10-21 - Session 3

The “cloudstone” the fills the mine is quite unusual and is very resistant to mining
There is a blight that is “killing” the miners. The mine is still exposing new miners to this risk.
The undead nightshift is now on strike
There are “tommyknockers” in the mine sabotaging the miners and their operations


Numyr appears to be cursed to be the new portal guardian for Catachthona in place of the owlbear - 2025-11-11 - Session 6

The killmoulis mentioned a Troll Witch (trol witch) - 2026-01-06 - Session 10
Gravity confirmed that Catachthona is the Troll Witch - 2026-01-06 - Session 10
Pinky said the Troll Witch lives in the Mortal Realm - 2026-01-13 - Session 11
The killmoulis says the Troll Witch is in the mountains with trolls and big horns 2026-01-27 - Session 13
Sheriff Catherine suggested the Bighorn Mountains, which are 3 days travel to the NW.
The killmoulis confirms that location - 2026-02-17 - Session 14
The Troll Witch offers three ways to end the curse, but the most promising option seems to be to rescue Mora and transfer it to her - 2026-02-24 - Session 15


Cecil has an alchemical recipe that would allow the party to conveniently transport their mounts when necessary. - 2025-12-10 - Session 8.  The recipe requires three special ingredients:

basilisk bile or droppings (available in Old Krorg’s cave)
a very large egg (Old Max says axebeaks nest in the Sandhills NE of Chugwater)
giant spider webbing (the gemkin nervously mentioned something about spiders - 2026-01-13 - Session 11)


Pinky’s prophesy from the Forking Path provided two choices.  Now that the king has fallen to Gravity, it seems you’re on the path of the first stanza. There are a couple things referenced in the prophesy (2026-01-13 - Session 11) and then echoed by the gremkin (2026-01-19 - Session 12) that introduced some related threads:

Evernight - see thread below
Queen of Night - see thread below
Nightmare Brigade - see thread below


The Evernight is a dark place, connected to shadow, that seems to be the source of some dark local influence

Prophesy: “And so shall wait the queen of night / In her starlit lands, far from sight.” - 2026-01-13 - Session 11
Gremkin:  The larger door of the warren leads to Evernight - 2026-01-19 - Session 12

You passed through shadow to reach the gremkin. Is that the same as crossing into Evernight?


(Children of Deadbridge: Old Nedward said they’d been pulled into Evernight, which was why they could see faeries and a starry night sky at day - 2026-03-17 - Interlude 1C)

The children then had to pass through shadow to reach the Shaggy Man. There were no stars in that dark mirror place.




A vaguely named Queen of Night seems to be pulling some strings lately

Pinky said someone recently set the basilisk to guard this side of the portal, which is odd. It’s possible that she is responsible.  She has a name that Pinky refuses to speak aloud here “even if it’s not her real one”. - 2026-01-13 - Session 11
Prophesy: “And so shall wait the queen of night / In her starlit lands, far from sight.” - 2026-01-13 - Session 11
Gremkin: His aged advisor, Pogus, explained that the former Grand Gremkin collected the sol fires for the ruler of Evernight, whose name they do not speak aloud here.  - 2026-01-19 - Session 12
(Children of Deadbridge: A fairy commented that Nocturna will be unhappy to have human children wandering in the Evernight… or perhaps very happy. - 2026-03-03 - Interlude 1A)


The Nightmare Brigade is involved in the sol-fire theft and hunts children

Prophesy: “The nightmares shall be held at bay / Until a child dies to those dark fey” - 2026-01-13 - Session 11
Gremkin: The Nightmare Brigade was to come collect the sol fires - 2026-01-19 - Session 12
(Children of Deadbridge: The children were haunted by a bogeyman tied to sleep, darkness, and nightmare. They killed it… but was it acting alone? - 2026-03-03 - Interlude 1A, 2026-03-10 - Interlude 1B, 2026-03-17 - Interlude 1C)


While riding to the Bighorn Mountains, it seems like the region north of Black Forest Hole is the center of a growing darkness - 2026-02-17 - Session 14

An undead gnoll wandered out of the area
The prairie dogs warrens to the west have filled with a consuming (possibly plane-crossing?) darkness
The local orcs hold that humans coming to their lands bring death with them.
